name: sounddevice (Real-Time Audio I/O)

auth.notes: Local library; wraps PortAudio C library

performance.notes: PortAudio backend; callback-based for low-latency real-time audio

agent_readiness.notes: Real-time audio I/O via PortAudio. import sounddevice as sd. sd.play(data, samplerate). sd.record(duration, samplerate=44100) → numpy array. sd.playrec(data, samplerate=44100, channels=2). sd.wait(). Callback: def callback(indata, frames, time, status): outdata[:] = indata. with sd.Stream(callback=callback): sd.sleep(10000). sd.query_devices() → list of devices. sd.default.device = 'My Audio Device'. Async: with sd.InputStream() as stream: frames, t, status = stream.read(1024). Works with numpy arrays. asyncio support via sd.InputStream.

gotchas: Requires PortAudio installed (included in wheels on most platforms)
Real-time callback must not allocate memory or block — no Python I/O
Device indices can differ between platforms and after hardware changes
Latency depends on OS and driver — not guaranteed sub-millisecond
No audio format conversion — must match device's supported formats
best_when: Playing and recording audio in real-time (microphone input, speaker output)
Low-latency audio processing with callback pattern
Interactive audio applications (speech recognition input, audio effects in real-time)
When numpy arrays should flow directly to/from audio hardware
avoid_when: Reading/writing audio files (use soundfile or librosa)
Server-side headless audio processing without hardware
summary: sounddevice provides real-time audio I/O via PortAudio. sd.play(data, sr). sd.record(dur, sr). Callback: def cb(indata, outdata, frames, time, status). sd.Stream(callback=cb). sd.query_devices(). asyncio support. MIT by SpatialAudio. Best for microphone recording and speaker playback with numpy arrays. Callback must not block. Device indices can change. No audio file format conversion.
